findings.title: Public-skill doc assertion is brittle and can miss legacy $review regressions
findings.rationale: The test collects bullet lines and then uses exact-string membership checks (`- `$close``, `- `$review-loop``, and not `- `$review``). This is brittle to harmless formatting changes and, more importantly, it does not reliably enforce legacy review absence: a line like `- `$review` (legacy alias)` would pass the negative check even though `$review` reappeared in the public skill surface.
findings.suggested_next_action: Parse the first backticked skill token from each bullet and assert token-set semantics (contains `close` and `review-loop`, excludes `review`) instead of exact full-line equality.
